applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to wrap the reference SwinTransformerV2 model. Level 2 optimization providing 2.30x speedup through Inductor graph optimization and kernel fusion.

top_bottlenecks: Elementwise ops (45.7%): fused addmm+relu, addmm+bmm+clamp kernels from torch.compile
GEMM (45.6%): rocBLAS Cijk_Alik_Bljk kernels for matrix multiplications
Attention softmax (5.4%): softmax and attention bias operations
Window ops (3.1%): permute+roll+view operations for window partition/reverse

notes: Stage 1 Complete: Baseline established with torch.compile
Final metrics: 3.38ms vs 7.76ms reference (2.30x speedup, score 73.0)
torch.compile autotune selected rocBLAS mm kernels for most GEMM operations
Profile shows Elementwise (45.7%) and GEMM (45.6%) dominate GPU time - 91.3% combined
Elementwise includes fused kernels from torch.compile: addmm+relu, addmm+bmm+clamp, softmax operations
Window partition/reverse operations (permute+roll+view) account for ~3% - Swin-specific overhead
Future optimization candidates: custom LayerNorm Triton kernel, fused MLP kernel, window operation Triton kernel
The torch.compile approach is working well - manual Triton kernels would need to beat the fused kernels torch.compile already generates
